request.question: Who won the 2016 Russian national silver medal with another Russian ice dancer born 29 April 1995?

title: Ivan Bukin
Alla Loboda
Alexandra Stepanova
Ksenia Monko
Nikita Katsalapov
Angelina Melnikova
Kirill Khaliavin
Pavel Drozd
Luciano Milo
Victoria Sinitsina
sentences: Ivan Andreyevich Bukin (Russian: Иван Андреевич Букин ; born 16 September 1993) is a Russian ice dancer.

With partner Alexandra Stepanova, he is the 2015 European bronze medalist, a three-time medalist on the Grand Prix series, a two-time Finlandia Trophy champion, and a three-time Russian national medalist (2017 silver, 2015 and 2016 bronze).
On the junior level, they won the 2013 World Junior Championships, 2012–13 JGP Final, and 2014 Russian Junior Championships.
Alla Andreyevna Loboda (Russian: Алла Андреевна Лобода ; born 10 December 1998) is a Russian ice dancer.

With her skating partner, Pavel Drozd, she is a two-time World Junior medalist (silver in 2017, bronze in 2016), a three-time JGP Final silver medalist (2014–15, 2015–16, 2016–17), and the 2016 Russian junior national champion.
Alexandra Nikolayevna Stepanova (Russian: Александра Николаевна Степанова ; born 19 August 1995) is a Russian ice dancer.

With partner Ivan Bukin, she is the 2015 European bronze medalist, a three-time medalist on the Grand Prix series, a two-time Finlandia Trophy champion (2014, 2016), and a three-time Russian national medalist (2017 silver, 2015 and 2016 bronze).
On the junior level, they won the 2013 World Junior Championships, 2012–13 JGP Final, and 2014 Russian Junior Championships.
Ksenia Ivanovna Monko (Russian: Ксения Ивановна Монько ; born 8 February 1992) is a former competitive ice dancer for Russia.
Alongside Kirill Khaliavin, she is the 2011 World Junior champion, a two-time (2009–10, 2010–11) Junior Grand Prix Final champion, and the 2015 Russian national silver medalist.
Nikita Gennadyevich Katsalapov (Russian: Никита Геннадьевич Кацалапов ; born 10 July 1991) is a Russian ice dancer.
With Victoria Sinitsina, he is the 2016 Russian national silver medalist.
Angelina Romanovna Melnikova (Russian: Ангелина Романовна Мельникова , born 18 July 2000) is a Russian artistic gymnast.
She is the 2014 European junior all-around, team and balance beam champion and uneven bars silver medalist.
She represented Russia at the 2016 Summer Olympics and won a silver medal in the team competition.
She is a 2016 European champion in the team event, and 2017 European champion in the floor exercise.
Melnikova is also the 2016 Russian national all-around champion and 2014 Russian junior all-around national champion.
Kirill Leonidovich Khaliavin (Russian: Кирилл Леонидович Халявин ; born 21 November 1990) is a Russian ice dancer who currently represents Spain.
With former partner Ksenia Monko, he is the 2011 World Junior champion, a two-time (2009–10, 2010–11) Junior Grand Prix Final champion, and the 2015 Russian national silver medalist.
With current partner Sara Hurtado, he is the 2016 Spanish Champion.
Pavel Igorevich Drozd (Russian: Павел Игоревич Дрозд ; born 14 December 1995) is a Russian ice dancer.

With his skating partner, Alla Loboda, he is a two-time World Junior medalist (silver in 2017, bronze in 2016), a three-time JGP Final silver medalist (2014–15, 2015–16, 2016–17), and the 2016 Russian junior national champion.
Luciano Milo (born 29 April 1980 in Rome) is an Italian former ice dancer.
He had the most success with partner Federica Faiella.
With Faiella, he is the 2000 Italian national silver medalist, two time World Junior silver medalist, and 1997/1998 Junior Grand Prix Final Champion.
They ended their partnership after the 1999/2000 season, when they placed 11th at the European Figure Skating Championships.
Milo then teamed up with Gloria Agogliati and skated with her for one season.
They won the bronze medal at the 2001 Italian Nationals.
Victoria Alexandrovna Sinitsina (Russian: Виктория Александровна Синицина ; born 29 April 1995) is a Russian ice dancer.
With Nikita Katsalapov, she is the 2016 Russian national silver medalist.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Who won the 2016 Russian national silver medal with another Russian ice dancer born 29 April 1995?
hop1_docs: Victoria Sinitsina | Victoria Alexandrovna Sinitsina (Russian: Виктория Александровна Синицина ; born 29 April 1995) is a Russian ice dancer.  With Nikita Katsalapov, she is the 2016 Russian national silver medalist.

Pavel Drozd | Pavel Igorevich Drozd (Russian: Павел Игоревич Дрозд ; born 14 December 1995) is a Russian ice dancer.  With his skating partner, Alla Loboda, he is a two-time World Junior medalist (silver in 2017, bronze in 2016), a three-time JGP Final silver medalist (2014–15, 2015–16, 2016–17), and the 2016 Russian junior national champion.

Alexandra Stepanova | Alexandra Nikolayevna Stepanova (Russian: Александра Николаевна Степанова ; born 19 August 1995) is a Russian ice dancer.  With partner Ivan Bukin, she is the 2015 European bronze medalist, a three-time medalist on the Grand Prix series, a two-time Finlandia Trophy champion (2014, 2016), and a three-time Russian national medalist (2017 silver, 2015 and 2016 bronze).  On the junior level, they won the 2013 World Junior Championships, 2012–13 JGP Final, and 2014 Russian Junior Championships.

Kirill Aleshin | Kirill Pavlovich Aleshin (Russian: Кирилл Павлович Алёшин ; born 24 April 1997) is a Russian ice dancer.  With partner Anastasia Skoptsova, he won two medals at the 2016 Winter Youth Olympics – bronze in ice dancing and gold in the team event.

Valeria Zenkova | Valeria Maximovna Zenkova (Russian: Валерия Максимовна Зенкова ; born 7 April 1995) is a Russian ice dancer.  With partner Valerie Sinitsin, she won five ISU Junior Grand Prix medals, including one gold, and placed fourth at the 2013 World Junior Championships.  They are the 2013 Russian junior champions.

Egor Bazin | Egor Yuryevich Bazin (Russian: Егор Юрьевич Базин , born 13 September 1995) is a Russian ice dancer.  With partner Sofia Evdokimova, he is the 2017 Winter Universiade silver medalist and 2016 Ice Star bronze medalist.

Lyudmila Yurlova | Lyudmila Petrovna Yurlova (Russian: Людмила Петровна Юрлова , 1 April 1972 – 28 November 2016) was a Russian ice hockey forward.  She was part of the national teams that won a silver medal at the 1996 European Championships and a bronze at the 2001 World Championships.
response.summary_1:
Primary topic: Victoria Sinitsina — 2016 Russian national silver (ice dancing)

- Victoria Alexandrovna Sinitsina — "born 29 April 1995" (passage [1]).
- Passage [1] states: "With Nikita Katsalapov, she is the 2016 Russian national silver medalist." — partner entity: Nikita Katsalapov.
- Ambiguity/missing facts: none in the passages — only Victoria Sinitsina matches the 29 April 1995 birthdate; no alternate candidates.
- Next-hop retrieval priority: No additional retrieval needed (passages unambiguously link the 29 Apr 1995 ice dancer to the 2016 silver and to Nikita Katsalapov).
response.hop2_query: Victoria Sinitsina Nikita Katsalapov 2016 Russian nationals silver partnership
hop2_docs: Nikita Katsalapov | Nikita Gennadyevich Katsalapov (Russian: Никита Геннадьевич Кацалапов ; born 10 July 1991) is a Russian ice dancer.  With Victoria Sinitsina, he is the 2016 Russian national silver medalist.

Victoria Sinitsina | Victoria Alexandrovna Sinitsina (Russian: Виктория Александровна Синицина ; born 29 April 1995) is a Russian ice dancer.  With Nikita Katsalapov, she is the 2016 Russian national silver medalist.

Nikita Nagornyy | Nikita Vladimirovich Nagornyy (Russian: Никита Владимирович Нагорный , born 12 February 1997 in Rostov-on-Don, Rostov Oblast, Russia) is a Russian artistic gymnast.  He is the 2016 Olympics Team silver medalist, the 2016 European Champion on floor, 2015 European Champion on vault, the 2014 Youth Olympic All-around silver medalist and pommel horse, rings and parallel bars Champion, and the 2014 European Junior vault champion.
Katsalapov | Katsalapov (Russian: Кацалапов ) is a Russian masculine surname, its feminine counterpart is Katsalapova.  It may refer to
Aleksandr Katsalapov | Aleksandr Anatolyevich Katsalapov (Russian: Александр Анатольевич Кацалапов ; born 5 April 1986) is a Russian footballer.  He plays for FC Ararat Moscow.

Nikita Lobintsev | Nikita Konstantinovich Lobintsev (Russian: Никита Константинович Лобинцев ; born 21 November 1988) is a Russian freestyle swimmer, who won the silver medal in the 4 × 200 m freestyle relay at the 2008 Summer Olympics.

Nikita Volodin | Nikita Andreyevich Volodin (Russian: Никита Андреевич Володин , born 29 June 1999) is a Russian pair skater.  With partner Alina Ustimkina, he is the 2016 CS Tallinn Trophy champion and 2016 Youth Olympic bronze medalist.
response.summary_2: Victoria Sinitsina (born 29 April 1995) is the 2016 Russian national silver medalist in ice dancing with Nikita Katsalapov. Nikita Katsalapov is a Russian ice dancer born 10 July 1991.
